results.title: PixFit: Turn 1 creative into every ad format, instantly - Product Hunt
results.content:
On text-heavy creatives: this is the key difference. We don't do a straight proportional scale. The flow actually understands the creative and recomposes it: it reads the individual elements and re-lays them out for the new format instead of stretching whatever was already there. Text gets the same treatment - it's understood as text, kept in the same typography, and reflowed to fit the new dimensions rather than squished or scaled into something that breaks the layout.

So the short answer: it reflows and adapts, it doesn't just scale everything proportionally. That's the whole point - keeping every version looking intentionally designed for its format, not warped.

Thanks for digging into the details! This is exactly the kind of questions and comments we love getting. 🙌 [...] Looking forward to trying it out! 🙌

Shubham Bhattacharji

resizing ad creatives across formats is genuinely one of those tasks that eats hours and adds zero creative value. the platform safe zones detail is what makes this useful, anyone can crop an image but knowing where tiktok puts its UI overlays vs where meta does is the actual knowledge being automated. how does it handle text-heavy creatives where a straight resize would break the layout? does it reflow elements or just scale everything proportionally?

Marco Bertone
PixFit

PixFit

@shubham4real   
Really good question, and you nailed exactly why we built the tool. [...] The painful part: once you had the creative asset approved (what we call master asset) and ready to run, making the 8 adaptations took roughly as long as making the original. Multiply that across every campaign, across every client, and the effort scales exponentially - for work that adds zero creative value.

results.title: Creatopy Reviews (2026) - Product Hunt
results.content:
•1 review

This tool exceeded my expectations time and time again. I have been using it for the past year, and I am extremely happy with it. What I appreciate most about this tool is that it's an all-in-one ad-making platform that allows you to create static or animated ads, generate sets of ads for all the major advertising platforms and social networks, and even publish your campaigns without leaving their platform. Plus, it's incredibly easy to grasp as it's got a super intuitive dashboard, which makes things easier for people like me with zero design background. And if you're part of a bigger team, you'll appreciate their team collaboration tools. I highly recommend it for its capabilities to create animated ads and to produce large sets of ads fast (with minimal human intervention). [...] Report

 12 views

The Brief

The Brief

•5 reviews

It's a versatile product for many use cases, you can make ad sets at once, different versions with feed, you can make simpler or complex animations very easily, you can have brand management with company colors, templates and you have the Ad serving option as well. And many other interesting features. It is an all-in-one tool for your ad campaigns.

results.title: AdCreative.ai Reviews (2026) - Product Hunt
results.content:
Instead of starting from scratch, I can quickly generate multiple creatives and test them right away. It’s especially useful when campaigns start to fatigue and you need fresh variations fast. Some outputs feel a bit generic, but a few are surprisingly good and only need minor tweaks before launching.

Overall, it’s a solid tool for saving time and scaling creative testing without relying too much on manual design work.

#### What needs improvement

There are a few areas that could be improved:

Some creatives feel repetitive if you generate multiple batches

Better control over brand voice and style would help

The scoring system doesn’t always match real performance

Would love to see stronger support for video creatives [...] AdCreative.ai

Report

 2 views [...] 4.7

Based on 11 reviews

Review Air?

Reviewers largely see Air as an easy, visually clear workspace for creative teams that centralizes assets, feedback, and collaboration better than tools like Google Drive or generic cloud storage. They repeatedly praise fast onboarding, intuitive navigation, strong search, visual search, previews, tagging, and flexible organization across photos, video, and other files. It seems especially useful for small marketing teams, nonprofits, freelancers, and multi-brand workflows. The main caveat is video-heavy use: one reviewer says processing is slow, sharing from desktop tools is less smooth, and the Premiere plugin feels clunky.
